subject: Accounting
title: The Laffer Curve
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Laffer Curve.

hook.text: Economist Arthur Laffer drew a curve on a napkin that changed tax policy forever.

buildup.text: The idea: at 0% tax, revenue is zero. At 100% tax, no one works—so revenue is also zero.

discovery.text: Somewhere in between is an optimal rate that maximizes revenue. That's the Laffer Curve.

twist.text: Politicians love citing it to justify tax cuts, but no one agrees on where the peak actually is.

climax.text: The curve is more concept than formula—useful for debate but nearly impossible to pinpoint.

punchline.text: The curve is famous. The optimal rate is still unknown.

quiz.question: What does the Laffer Curve illustrate?
options: There's an optimal tax rate that maximizes revenue
Tax cuts always pay for themselves
